verdict: 2026-07-07 11:06:31 PDT — The main shape is sound: a pure `client/src/lib/rateTable.js` helper module, `normalizeName` reuse, Vitest coverage, no new dependency, and keeping React keys as opaque row ids all fit the repo. I would not build the hydration/id-repair exactly as sketched, and the alias policy needs a decision before implementation.

findings.title: Dirty hydration repair is never persisted
findings.claim: The spec says hydration repair fixes data already on the server, but the sketch runs `repairConfig` before `hydratedRef` flips specifically so it does not save. That heals the in-memory table for the current session, but Thomas's corrupted server row set remains corrupted until some later unrelated edit happens to save the whole config.
findings.alternative: Have `repairConfig` return either `{ config, changed }` or preserve identity for clean input, then perform one best-effort `saveRates(adapter, repairedConfig)` only when the loaded server config was actually repaired. Clean loads still skip the echo save.
findings.win: Existing corrupted saved data is actually cleaned once, while preserving the no-redundant-save invariant for clean configs.

findings.title: Global renumbering makes stable ids positional
findings.claim: The in-scope invariant calls the row id a persistent unique handle, not a positional index, but `dedupeRateTable` is sketched as renumbering ids to sequential strings after collapse. That needlessly churns otherwise valid legacy ids and makes the repaired ids a function of current order rather than preserving opaque handles.
findings.alternative: Repair minimally: after name dedupe, keep the first occurrence's existing id when it is present and unique; assign `nextId(rows)` only to missing or colliding ids. Treat any unique string id as clean, regardless of prefix or gaps.
findings.win: The uniqueness bug is fixed without avoidable persisted-id migration, row-key churn, or extra dirty repairs for data that was already structurally valid.

findings.title: Alias cap/upsert policy is still unresolved
findings.claim: The sketch leaves alias upsert/cap conditional on an open question, while AC5 says alias behavior is per the resolved question. Without deciding this before implementation, the helper API, UI disabled state, and tests can all land in a half-scoped shape.
findings.alternative: Resolve the policy in the design before Claude builds. If symmetry is desired, implement `upsertAlias` by normalized alias plus the same `MAX_RATES` cap; otherwise explicitly scope aliases to id generation and id repair only.
findings.win: The implementation gets one clear alias contract and avoids reworking tests/UI after the first pass.
